symbols: top hat and formal attire — the social respectability and bourgeois milieu of the subject
profile view facing right — forward-looking orientation, determination and purpose
walking stick or cane — the gentleman's bearing and social status
description: A charcoal and conte crayon drawing showing the profile portrait of Paul Signac, the Neo-Impressionist painter and friend of Seurat. Signac is depicted in right profile, wearing a tall top hat and a high-collared coat or overcoat. He has a short beard and mustache. A walking stick or cane is visible behind him. The drawing employs Seurat's characteristic technique of building tones through dense, soft strokes of crayon on textured paper, creating subtle gradations of light and shadow. The background is rendered in vertical hatching, creating a sense of atmosphere around the figure.
